raw_response:
{
  "communicative_function": "The statement expresses a personal resolution to adopt a more balanced, nuanced perspective when experiencing a rigid, black-and-white mindset.",
  "implicit_claims": [
    "The speaker sometimes experiences an all-or-nothing frame of mind.",
    "In that frame, there is a tendency to ignore partial truths on either side.",
    "Partial truths exist on both sides of an issue.",
    "The speaker believes that seeking partial truths can be a corrective to all-or-nothing thinking."
  ],
  "coherence_assessment": "The statement is internally coherent: it acknowledges a cognitive tendency and announces a deliberate shift in approach, which is logically consistent.",
  "conditions_for_usefulness": "Useful when the speaker is genuinely in an all-or-nothing frame and is willing to pause and reflect; also useful when the context involves polarized debates where partial truths on both sides are accessible.",
  "conditions_for_misleading": "Could be misleading if the all-or-nothing frame is actually justified (e.g., when one side is entirely false or harmful), or if the search for partial truth leads to false equivalence or moral relativism in contexts where clear distinctions are necessary."
}

parsed_json.communicative_function: The statement expresses a personal resolution to adopt a more balanced, nuanced perspective when experiencing a rigid, black-and-white mindset.
implicit_claims: The speaker sometimes experiences an all-or-nothing frame of mind.
In that frame, there is a tendency to ignore partial truths on either side.
Partial truths exist on both sides of an issue.
The speaker believes that seeking partial truths can be a corrective to all-or-nothing thinking.
parsed_json.coherence_assessment: The statement is internally coherent: it acknowledges a cognitive tendency and announces a deliberate shift in approach, which is logically consistent.
parsed_json.conditions_for_usefulness: Useful when the speaker is genuinely in an all-or-nothing frame and is willing to pause and reflect; also useful when the context involves polarized debates where partial truths on both sides are accessible.
parsed_json.conditions_for_misleading: Could be misleading if the all-or-nothing frame is actually justified (e.g., when one side is entirely false or harmful), or if the search for partial truth leads to false equivalence or moral relativism in contexts where clear distinctions are necessary.
